---
title: "Master Bottleneck"
author: "Jacob Bauer"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
# MASTER BOTTLENECK FUNCTION
# target: a variable containing "all", "blue", "brown", or "green"
master_bottleneck <- function(population, pct_remove, target) {
  
  if (target == "all") {
    # Logic for a general population crash
    n <- length(population)
    num_remove <- floor(n * pct_remove)
    remove_indices <- sample(seq_len(n), size = num_remove)
    result <- population[-remove_indices]
    
  } else {
    # Logic for a specific allele being targeted
    # Here, 'target' will be the specific string (e.g., "blue") chosen by the user
    target_indices <- which(population == target)
    safe_indices <- which(population != target)
    
    n_target <- length(target_indices)
    num_remove <- floor(n_target * pct_remove)
    
    if (num_remove > 0) {
      remove_targets <- sample(target_indices, size = num_remove)
      surviving_target_indices <- setdiff(target_indices, remove_targets)
    } else {
      surviving_target_indices <- target_indices
    }
    
    result <- population[c(surviving_target_indices, safe_indices)]
  }
  
  return(data.frame(result = result, pct_removed = pct_remove))
}
```
